summary: Enterprise service desk mobile app for Reliable Home Appliance Repair LLC. Single canister ICP app with Motoko backend and responsive mobile-first frontend. Owner principal: q5rzs-s67ph-qtb5w-e66j5-2iqax-vlwa5-5pqxy-yosti-xhcis-ocfw6-yqe (app-derived principal). Ryan is the authorized user with CRUD abilities. Owner has full access confirmed. Building incrementally: core job/client management, role-based access control, labor rates/inventory on jobs, on-screen signature blob storage, and job photo capture (open camera). Professional Green & silver theme. Labor Rates and Inventory accessible under Dashboard. Owner has exclusive access to change labor rates and view metrics.
architectural_notes: Single canister ICP app: Motoko backend + responsive mobile-first frontend
Roles: stable var owner Principal + TrieMap<Principal, Role> where Role = {#owner, #admin, #authorized}. hasPermission checks owner first then map.
Only #owner can add/remove roles or change settings. #owner + #admin + #authorized get full CRUD on business data.
All stable storage: clients, jobs, inventory as stable vars/TrieMaps. Fully upgrade-safe.
Offline queue via localStorage sync for crash-proof mobile use.

Owner principal updated to app-derived principal q5rzs-s67ph-qtb5w-e66j5-2iqax-vlwa5-5pqxy-yosti-xhcis-ocfw6-yqe. Internet Identity generates a different derived principal per canister — the NNS principal (w3w26-...) differs from the in-app principal.

Owner privileges loss is a recurring issue — likely caused by owner principal mismatch between hardcoded stable var and the app-derived Internet Identity principal. Must verify Principal.fromText initialization and that hasPermission checks owner stable var directly (not via roles TrieMap).
known_issues: Initial full prompt was too complex to process in one pass — building incrementally starting with core job/client/roles.
Build failed twice when trying to add labor rates + on-screen signatures + appliance type in one pass — retry with smaller incremental prompts.
Signature screen on estimates and Stripe integration not visible in build — needs re-implementation or fix.

Owner loses privileges after certain builds — root cause: possible overwrite of stable owner principal or mismatch between NNS principal and app-derived principal. Reverting to version 8 to troubleshoot.
Invoice title shows wrong business name — must be changed to 'Reliable Home Appliance Repair LLC'.
Labor rates cannot be added manually/custom on job detail — only predefined rates supported currently.
Google Review URL may be causing code instability — candidate for removal if it breaks builds.

existing_features.title: Build a responsive mobile-first React frontend with a professional green and silver visual theme. Use a deep forest green primary color, silver/light gray accents, and clean sans-serif typography. Apply this theme consistently across all screens, cards, buttons, and navigation. The layout should use a bottom navigation bar on mobile with tabs: Dashboard, Clients, Jobs, and Settings.

Build a Clients screen that lists all clients with a search/filter input. Each client card shows name, phone, and address. Include an Add Client button that opens a form to create a new client (name, phone, address, notes, email, googleReviewUrl fields). Tapping a client opens an Edit/Detail view with all fields editable and a Delete option. If googleReviewUrl is set, show a 'Request Google Review' button that opens that URL.

Build a Jobs screen that lists all jobs grouped by status (Open, In Progress, Complete). Each job card shows the associated client name, job date, status badge, and tech principal (truncated). Include an Add Job button that opens a form to create a new job (select client by name, date, notes, waiver type, maintenance package). Tapping a job opens a detail view showing all fields, with the ability to update status and notes. Include a Delete option.
Build a Dashboard screen that displays summary metrics: total open jobs, total jobs, and a count of jobs by status. Show a list of recent jobs (last 5) with client name, status, and date.

Build a Settings screen that is only accessible to the #owner role. Display the current caller's principal. Show a role management section where the owner can input a principal text and assign it the #authorized role, and view or remove existing authorized principals.

Add an Inventory screen to the frontend under a new 'Inventory' tab in the bottom navigation bar. The screen lists all parts with name, SKU, quantity, and a low-stock warning badge when quantity is at or below the lowStockThreshold. Include an Add Part button that opens a form (name, SKU, quantity, lowStockThreshold, unitCost). Tapping a part opens an edit/detail view with all fields editable and a Delete option. Include a 'Use on Job' action on the detail view that lets the user specify a job and quantity to deduct.

Add a Scheduling / Calendar screen to the frontend. Display jobs in a weekly calendar view where each day column shows jobs scheduled for that day (using scheduledStart). Tapping a job navigates to its detail page. Include controls to navigate between weeks. On the job detail page, add fields to set scheduledStart and scheduledEnd using a date-time picker.

Add an Invoice/Estimate screen to the frontend. From a job detail page, include a 'Generate Invoice' button that opens a full-screen invoice preview styled in the professional green and silver theme, showing: business name ('Reliable Home Appliance Repair LLC'), client name and address, job date, job notes, parts used (from inventory usePartOnJob records), estimate amount, and tech name. Include a 'Download PDF' button that uses html2canvas + jsPDF to export the invoice as a PDF file.

Add a Payments section to the job detail page. Display the current stripePaymentId if set. Include a form to enter a Stripe Payment ID manually (the ID obtained externally from Stripe) and save it to the job via the updateJobPayment backend endpoint. Show a payment status badge (Paid / Unpaid) based on whether a stripePaymentId is present.

Update the Settings screen (owner-only) to include: a Stripe Secret Key input field that saves via setStripeKey and shows a masked current value if one is stored; a Maintenance Packages management section where the owner can add, edit, and delete packages (name, description, intervalDays, price).
Update the Add Job and Job Detail forms to include a Maintenance Package selector that lists available packages from the backend (listPackages) and allows the user to assign one to the job.

Add a Labor Rates management section to the Settings screen (owner-only). Display the current list of labor rates. Allow the owner to add a new rate (name, type: hourly or flat, amount in dollars), edit an existing rate inline, and delete a rate. Use the createLaborRate, listLaborRates, updateLaborRate, and deleteLaborRate backend endpoints.

On the Invoice preview screen, add a 'Share' button that uses the Web Share API (navigator.share) to share the invoice as an HTML file and/or PDF blob on supported devices. Fall back gracefully (hide the button or show a tooltip) on browsers that do not support navigator.share. The shared content should include the business name, client info, job details, parts used, labor rates applied, and estimate amount.

On the Scheduling/Calendar screen, display a note or badge on each scheduled job card indicating the job's last known location if available. Add a 'View on Map' link per job card that opens a Google Maps URL (https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=LAT,LNG or address) in a new browser tab using the job's client address or stored location. Do not integrate live GPS tracking (not supported), but surface the Google Maps deep-link as the closest available substitute.

Add a 'Labor Rates' quick-access card or section on the Dashboard page that is only visible to the #owner role. The card should display a summary of existing labor rates (name, type, amount) and include a button that navigates to the Labor Rates management section in Settings. If the caller is not #owner, this section must not render.

Add an 'Inventory' quick-access card or section on the Dashboard page visible to both #owner and #authorized roles. The card should display a summary count of total parts, number of low-stock items, and a button that navigates to the full Inventory screen. Low-stock count should be derived from listParts results where quantity <= lowStockThreshold.

Ensure the Dashboard metrics section (open jobs count, in-progress jobs count, complete jobs count, total clients count, and any revenue or summary statistics) is only visible to the #owner role. Authorized users navigating to the Dashboard should see jobs and inventory sections but not the owner-only metrics cards.

On the Job Detail page, add a 'Labor' section below the existing fields. Display existing labor line items for the job (labor rate name, type, hours if hourly, and computed amount). Include an 'Add Labor' button that opens an inline form: select a labor rate from listLaborRates, enter hours (if hourly type), and a description. Allow removing individual line items. Show a running subtotal for labor. Use the addLaborLineItem and removeLaborLineItem backend endpoints.

On the Job Detail page, add a 'Parts Used' section that displays parts already associated with the job via existing usePartOnJob records. Include an 'Add Part' button that opens an inline form: select a part from listParts, enter a quantity to deduct, and confirm. This calls the existing usePartOnJob endpoint. Show part name, SKU, quantity used, and unit cost per line. Display a running parts subtotal.

On the Login page (LoginPage.tsx) and/or the Profile Setup page (ProfileSetupPage.tsx), add an optional signature capture step after the user authenticates. Display a canvas-based signature pad where the user can draw their signature with a mouse or finger. Include Clear and Save buttons. On Save, convert the canvas drawing to a Blob and call storeUserSignature. Show a confirmation message when saved. Skip the step gracefully if the user dismisses it.

On the Job Detail page, add a 'Photos' section that displays thumbnail previews of all stored photos for the job. Include a 'Take Photo / Upload' button that uses an HTML file input with accept='image/*' and capture='environment' to open the device camera or file picker. On selection, read the file as an ArrayBuffer, convert to Blob, and call addJobPhoto. Display the uploaded photo as a thumbnail in the section. Include a delete icon on each thumbnail that calls removeJobPhoto.

On the Job Detail page, replace the 'Maintenance Package' field with a 'Labor' section. The Labor section must allow users to add fully customizable labor line items directly on the job — each line item has a name (free-text, not required to reference a saved labor rate), type (hourly or flat), hours (if hourly), rate amount (editable per line), and description. Display each line item with its computed amount and a remove button. Show a running labor subtotal. Persist labor line items via the existing addLaborLineItem and removeLaborLineItem backend endpoints, but allow the user to enter custom name/amount values not tied to a predefined rate. Remove the Maintenance Package selector from the Add Job and Job Detail forms entirely.

Maintenance Packages management remains available under the Settings screen (owner-only). Ensure the Settings page still contains the Maintenance Packages section (add, edit, delete packages with name, description, intervalDays, price) and that no reference to maintenance packages appears on any job form.

On the Invoice/Estimate preview page (InvoicePreviewPage.tsx), add an in-page signature capture section at the bottom of the invoice. Display a canvas-based signature pad where the customer or technician can draw a signature with a mouse or finger. Include 'Clear' and 'Save Signature' buttons. On save, convert the canvas drawing to a Blob and call storeUserSignature (or attach it to the job estimate record). Display a confirmation message when the signature is saved. The signature should render on the invoice preview and be included in the downloaded PDF.

Implement Stripe payment integration. In the Settings screen (owner-only), add a Stripe Secret Key input field that saves the key via the existing setStripeKey backend endpoint and displays a masked value if one is already stored. On the backend, add a createPaymentIntent(jobId: Nat, amountCents: Nat) endpoint restricted to #authorized that uses the stored Stripe secret key to create a Stripe Payment Intent via the Stripe API (https://api.stripe.com/v1/payment_intents) and returns the client secret. On the Job Detail page, add a 'Charge via Stripe' button that calls createPaymentIntent with the job's estimated total, then stores the returned payment intent ID on the job via updateJobPayment. Show a payment status badge (Paid / Unpaid) based on whether a stripePaymentId is present.

On the Client Detail page, if a Google Review URL is not already set for the client, pre-populate or suggest the URL 'https://www.google.com/search?q=reliablehomeapplianceny.com' as the default Google Review URL. Ensure the existing 'Request Google Review' button on the Client Detail page opens this URL (or the client's custom googleReviewUrl if set) in a new browser tab. Additionally, surface a 'Request Google Review' button or link on the Job Detail page that links to the associated client's Google review URL (defaulting to https://www.google.com/search?q=reliablehomeapplianceny.com if none is stored).

On the Invoice/Estimate preview page (InvoicePreviewPage.tsx), ensure the canvas-based signature capture section is visibly rendered at the bottom of the invoice. The section must display a signature pad canvas, 'Clear' and 'Save Signature' buttons, a confirmation message on successful save, and the saved signature image rendered inline in the invoice. The signature must also be captured in the downloaded PDF export. If the SignatureCapture component or useSignaturePad hook are not currently being used on this page, wire them in now.

On the Job Detail page (JobDetailPage.tsx), ensure the Stripe payment section is visibly rendered. It must include: a 'Charge via Stripe' button that calls the createPaymentIntent backend endpoint with the job's estimated total (sum of labor + parts), stores the returned payment intent ID via updateJobPayment, and shows a 'Paid' / 'Unpaid' payment status badge based on whether stripePaymentId is present. If the section is missing or not wired to the backend, re-implement it now using the existing createPaymentIntent and updateJobPayment endpoints.

On the Scheduling/Calendar screen (CalendarPage.tsx), add a cost summary overlay or info panel to each scheduled job card that displays the job's diagnostic fee (if set), labor rate subtotal (sum of labor line items), parts subtotal (sum of parts used), and a tax rate variable. The tax rate should be a configurable value (default 8.875%). Display the computed pre-tax total and post-tax total on the card or in an expandable detail section.

Add a 'Routing' section to the Scheduling/Calendar screen. Display a list of all open and in-progress jobs. Allow the user to drag and drop job cards into an ordered route list. Each job in the route list should show the client name, address, and a 'View on Map' link that opens a Google Maps directions URL (https://www.google.com/maps/dir/?api=1&destination=ADDRESS) in a new tab using the client's address. Display the ordered route as a numbered list. Persist the route order in local component state (no backend persistence required).

In the Scheduling/Calendar screen (CalendarPage.tsx), ensure all displayed times (scheduledStart, scheduledEnd, job dates) are formatted and labeled in Eastern Standard Time (EST / America/New_York). Use the Intl.DateTimeFormat API with timeZone: 'America/New_York' for all time rendering in the calendar view and week navigation labels.

Replace the default Google Review URL throughout the entire application (ClientDetailPage.tsx, JobDetailPage.tsx, and any other location where a Google Review URL default is referenced) with the following exact URL: https://www.google.com/search?client=safari&hs=bqlU&sca_esv=477fbb8c9f03b619&hl=en-us&biw=393&bih=695&sxsrf=ANbL-n4RtO1QhvuqwZK_dXDG4LEsJQO68g:1772317989694&q=www.appliancerepairwalden.com+reviews&uds=ALYpb_kZB5BOMUFlRqxCQelgPO46flvNFV4llOp9FXmtgN0bFd_59aFqnzrv2lL_22fdcKjO_3pVePgz54Y0AhVY-GXqXefnH1qx8tFczBBdwApAdYXTD4RJ2fBkcTn2WMvET_C7noP7FDKpQdsny6qiyHWQVVEzt_rbESpi998me2Rg_kwCVEN74ocI_4XPNR22msLdpmHbL8GYbInBlVUBwAcIKp_O7ifZcZcIuUQQZROm0YQtZPTCZ3SVevvuwibzruurIRfjpjjybvX9x_eguP-hCuWxArDZZrO09vSYhCpocS_cwWMGzY0Rwgw8zjgH5WGiY2fHfDs2UCkdDDuktP48v8L24SA8xc5Ab-2pYFO3nVVnJjMPVJpHFf0r559CWqf2c7qmL2DlIq9LVmUhwLfYHaubp-qPmWjWko6lXf2oCXBA71Y3Lp6zVnH89YUnvqoKlmf1B2-nVHIk0CujdXTh7wl4QKF-8kTjNbCWi5TusYLAhMY&si=AL3DRZEsmMGCryMMFSHJ3StBhOdZ2-6yYkXd_doETEE1OR-qOR1dDOzNahzjiSdI7VdJYDnJGXelcQqzXSy0Yqcso2TsW1Ly5cwaMJIgfN94_biTlzTkpCdTbGgRuaEYR62-qzW3A8vAeLXPm0nS58Y2B2F8ZX0rKA%3D%3D&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwiYrtGBn_2SAxVXE1kFHQkkMeYQk8gLegQIIhAB&ictx=1&stq=1&cs=0&lei=YG2jaeaXGK6l5NoPp7HaiAw#ebo=1 — This URL must be used as the fallback/default wherever a client has no custom googleReviewUrl stored.

In the frontend Settings page (SettingsPage.tsx), update the owner-check logic that gates the Settings screen. The frontend isOwner check must compare the authenticated Internet Identity principal against the hardcoded string 'q5rzs-s67ph-qtb5w-e66j5-2iqax-vlwa5-5pqxy-yosti-xhcis-ocfw6-yqe'. Do not rely solely on a backend role query that could return a stale or missing result. If the backend getMyRole (or equivalent) query is used, also verify locally. The Settings screen must render fully for the owner principal.

On the Invoice/Estimate preview page (InvoicePreviewPage.tsx), change the displayed business name / invoice title to exactly 'Reliable Home Appliance Repair LLC'. This string must appear as the primary heading on the printed/preview invoice and in the downloaded PDF export.

On the Job Detail page (JobDetailPage.tsx), ensure the Labor section supports fully manual/custom labor line item entry without requiring a predefined labor rate. The inline 'Add Labor' form must include: a free-text name field, a type selector (hourly or flat), an hours field (shown only when hourly is selected), an editable rate/amount field (dollars, not required to match any saved rate), and an optional description field. Each added line item must display its computed amount (hours × rate for hourly, flat amount for flat) and a remove button. Show a running labor subtotal below the list. Persist line items via the existing addLaborLineItem and removeLaborLineItem backend endpoints.

Add a configurable tax rate field to the job detail and invoice/estimate workflow. On the Job Detail page, display a tax rate input (percentage, default 8.875%) that is applied to the combined total of parts subtotal and labor subtotal to compute the tax amount and final post-tax total. Display the pre-tax subtotal, tax amount (labeled with the rate, e.g. 'Tax 8.875%'), and the post-tax total on both the Job Detail page and the Invoice/Estimate preview page. The tax rate value should be editable by the user on the job/invoice screen (not a global setting) and should be included in the PDF export.

Remove all Google Review URL references, buttons, default URL values, and related logic from the entire frontend codebase (ClientDetailPage.tsx, JobDetailPage.tsx, and any other file where a Google Review URL default, button, or link appears). If the Google Review feature is causing build instability, remove it completely rather than patching it. After removal, no 'Request Google Review' button, link, or URL constant should remain anywhere in the application.

Verify and ensure the job photo blob storage section on the Job Detail page (JobDetailPage.tsx) is fully operational. The 'Photos' section must display thumbnail previews of all stored job photos, include a 'Take Photo / Upload' button using an HTML file input with accept='image/*' and capture='environment', read the selected file as an ArrayBuffer, convert it to a Blob, and call addJobPhoto. Each thumbnail must include a delete icon that calls removeJobPhoto. If this section is not currently rendering or is broken, re-implement it using the existing addJobPhoto and removeJobPhoto backend endpoints.

Confirm the Stripe payment section on the Job Detail page (JobDetailPage.tsx) remains visible and functional after the current changes. The section must show a 'Charge via Stripe' button that calls createPaymentIntent with the job's post-tax total (parts + labor + tax), stores the returned payment intent ID via updateJobPayment, and displays a 'Paid' / 'Unpaid' badge based on whether stripePaymentId is present. Do not remove or regress this section during the other changes in this build.

new_feature_requests.summary: Core role-based access control: owner (stable var), admin, authorized roles via TrieMap. hasPermission helper. Only owner can manage roles/settings.
Client management: CRUD for clients with id, name, phone, address, notes, email, googleReviewUrl. List/search/add/edit UI.
Job management: CRUD for jobs with clientId, tech, date, status (open/inProgress/complete), notes, photos, estimate with signature, waiver type, custom fields.
Dashboard with metrics: open/total jobs, revenue YTD, tech logins. GPS map with active job pins via Google Maps embed.
Inventory management: parts list, add/edit/use-on-job (decrement qty), low-stock alerts.
Invoice/estimate workflow: PDF generation (html2canvas + jsPDF), green & silver professional layout, email/SMS share options, attach to client record.
Scheduling/Routing: calendar view, drag jobs to tech routes, GPS tracking, lastKnownLocation stored on job.
Stripe payment integration: owner-stored secret key, create/confirm payment intent from backend, payment ID stored on job. Support for multiple Stripe keys.
Maintenance packages: predefined and custom packages (e.g. Quarterly Laundry Maintenance), assignable to client/job.

Settings tab (owner only): add tech principal as #authorized, global rates, Stripe key input, email/SMS API key slots (Twilio/SendGrid). Industry switcher between Reliable Home Appliance Repair and Reliable Refrigeration & Appliance Repair.

Implement a single-actor Motoko backend with stable role-based access control. Store the owner principal in a stable var. Maintain a roles TrieMap<Principal, Role> where Role is a variant { #owner; #admin; #authorized }. Implement a hasPermission(caller: Principal, minRole: Role): Bool helper that checks owner first, then the map. Only the #owner principal can add or remove roles. All job and client CRUD operations require at minimum #authorized permission.

Implement stable storage and full CRUD backend functions for Clients. Each client record must have: id (Nat), name (Text), phone (Text), address (Text), notes (Text), email (optional Text), googleReviewUrl (optional Text). Expose createClient, getClient, listClients, updateClient, and deleteClient endpoints, all guarded by hasPermission(caller, #authorized).

Implement stable storage and full CRUD backend functions for Jobs. Each job record must have: id (Nat), clientId (Nat), tech (Principal), date (Time), status (variant { #open; #inProgress; #complete }), notes (Text), photos ([Blob]), estimate (optional { amount: Nat; sigData: Blob; sigTime: Time }), waiverType (optional variant { #preexisting; #potential; #general }), maintenancePackage (optional Text), stripePaymentId (optional Text). Expose createJob, getJob, listJobs, updateJob, updateJobStatus, and deleteJob endpoints, all guarded by hasPermission(caller, #authorized).

Extend the backend Job record to include a stripePaymentId (optional Text) field and add a Stripe configuration storage: allow the #owner to store and retrieve a Stripe secret key (stored as a stable var). Expose setStripeKey and getStripeKey endpoints restricted to #owner. Add updateJobPayment endpoint to store a stripePaymentId on a job, restricted to #authorized.

Add an Inventory module to the backend. Each part record must have: id (Nat), name (Text), sku (optional Text), quantity (Nat), lowStockThreshold (Nat), unitCost (optional Nat). Expose createPart, getPart, listParts, updatePart, deletePart, and usePartOnJob (decrements quantity by a given amount and records the jobId). All endpoints require at minimum #authorized permission.

Add a Maintenance Packages module to the backend. Each package record must have: id (Nat), name (Text), description (Text), intervalDays (Nat), price (optional Nat). Expose createPackage, listPackages, updatePackage, deletePackage endpoints restricted to #authorized. The existing job record's maintenancePackage field (optional Text) can reference a package name.

Add scheduling fields to the Job record: scheduledStart (optional Time) and scheduledEnd (optional Time). Expose a updateJobSchedule endpoint to set these fields, restricted to #authorized. Add a listJobsByDate query that returns all jobs whose scheduledStart falls within a given date range (startTime: Time, endTime: Time).
Scheduling/Calendar weekly view linked to live GPS tracker from Google Maps (show tech/job locations on map).
Labor rates: customizable labor rate settings (per hour or flat) stored in backend, editable from Settings screen.
Send estimates/invoices via HTML, PDF, or native device share (Web Share API). Attach to client/job record.

Add a Labor Rates module to the backend. Store a list of labor rate records, each with: id (Nat), name (Text), rateType (variant { #hourly; #flat }), and amount (Nat, in cents). Expose createLaborRate, listLaborRates, updateLaborRate, and deleteLaborRate endpoints restricted to #authorized. Store rates in a stable TrieMap.
Set owner principal to w3w26-hrxnk-kfpaw-7trqf-ngcqt-xm3i3-qebzp-vtech-eqcjs-nahkw-fae in stable var. Ryan is the authorized user with CRUD abilities.
Add Labor Rates section accessible under/from Dashboard. Only owner can change labor rates and view metrics.
Add Inventory section accessible under/from Dashboard.
On-screen signature capture on estimate/invoice screen (blob storage for signature).
Add appliance type dropdown menu to job creation/detail form.
Add labor line items to the job detail screen (select labor rate, hours/flat, add to job).
Blob storage for job photos on the job detail screen.

Update the backend stable owner principal variable to 'w3w26-hrxnk-kfpaw-7trqf-ngcqt-xm3i3-qebzp-vtech-eqcjs-nahkw-fae'. Ensure all hasPermission checks for #owner role use this principal. The authorized user (Ryan) retains #authorized role with CRUD access to clients, jobs, inventory, and other standard operations, but cannot change labor rates or view owner-only metrics.

Update backend stable owner principal to q5rzs-s67ph-qtb5w-e66j5-2iqax-vlwa5-5pqxy-yosti-xhcis-ocfw6-yqe (app-derived principal). This replaces the previously hardcoded NNS principal. All hasPermission #owner checks must use this new principal.

Update the stable owner principal variable in the Motoko backend to 'q5rzs-s67ph-qtb5w-e66j5-2iqax-vlwa5-5pqxy-yosti-xhcis-ocfw6-yqe'. This is the app-derived Internet Identity principal for the owner. All hasPermission checks for the #owner role must use this principal. Remove or replace any previously hardcoded principal value.

In backend/main.mo, update the stable owner principal variable to the exact text value 'q5rzs-s67ph-qtb5w-e66j5-2iqax-vlwa5-5pqxy-yosti-xhcis-ocfw6-yqe'. This must be set using Principal.fromText("q5rzs-s67ph-qtb5w-e66j5-2iqax-vlwa5-5pqxy-yosti-xhcis-ocfw6-yqe") in the stable var initializer. All hasPermission checks for the #owner role must compare the caller against this principal. Remove any previously hardcoded principal value. Do not rely on a roles TrieMap entry for owner — the owner check must be a direct equality comparison against this stable var so it cannot be overwritten.

In backend/main.mo, ensure that all inventory endpoints (createPart, getPart, listParts, updatePart, deletePart, usePartOnJob) correctly accept calls from principals with at minimum the #authorized role, and also from the #owner principal. Verify the hasPermission guard on every inventory function uses the updated owner principal so the owner can perform all inventory CRUD operations.

Extend the backend Job record to include a laborLineItems field: a list of records each containing laborRateId (Nat), rateType (variant { #hourly; #flat }), hours (optional Float), amount (Nat, in cents), and description (Text). Add addLaborLineItem and removeLaborLineItem endpoints on jobs restricted to #authorized. The job's total estimate amount should be derivable from the sum of labor line items plus parts used.

Extend the backend to store a signature blob associated with the login/onboarding flow. Add a storeUserSignature(sig: Blob) endpoint restricted to authenticated callers (minimum #authorized) that saves the signature blob keyed by the caller's principal in a stable TrieMap<Principal, Blob>. Add a getUserSignature() query that returns the stored blob for the caller. The signature should be associated with the user profile, not a specific job.

Extend the backend Job record to include a photos field: list of Blob (already present as [Blob] per prior spec). Add addJobPhoto(jobId: Nat, photo: Blob) and removeJobPhoto(jobId: Nat, photoIndex: Nat) endpoints restricted to #authorized. Ensure these persist in stable storage and are included in getJob and listJobs responses.

Move labor rates into the Job Detail page as a customizable 'Labor' section. Replace the 'Maintenance Package' field on jobs with a 'Labor' section. Maintenance packages remain available under Settings only.
Add a signature capture screen on the Estimates/Invoice page. Display a canvas-based signature pad, allow the user to sign, and store the signature blob associated with the estimate/job.
Stripe payment integration: allow owner to store Stripe secret key in Settings, create/confirm payment intents from backend, store payment ID on job, show payment status badge on job detail.
Add a 'Request Google Review' button linking to reliablehomeapplianceny.com Google review URL on client detail and/or job detail page.
Add diagnostic fee, labor rate, parts, and tax rate variables to the live map display on the scheduling/routing screen.
Add a routing section to the scheduling screen with drag-and-drop job assignment to map. Scheduling section timezone set to EST.
Replace the Google Review URL default with https://www.google.com/search?client=safari&...#ebo=1 (full appliancerepairwalden.com reviews link) for all client/job detail pages.

In backend/main.mo, declare the owner principal as a stable let constant (not a mutable stable var) initialized with Principal.fromText("q5rzs-s67ph-qtb5w-e66j5-2iqax-vlwa5-5pqxy-yosti-xhcis-ocfw6-yqe"). This value must never be overwritten by any upgrade, migration, or role-management call. Remove any stable var or TrieMap entry that could shadow or replace the owner principal.

In backend/main.mo, rewrite the hasPermission helper so that #owner checks are a direct equality comparison between the caller and the stable owner constant (REQ-48) and never fall through to the roles TrieMap. The roles TrieMap is only consulted for #authorized and #admin role checks. Ensure no code path can grant #owner via the TrieMap.

Audit and fix every owner-guarded backend endpoint (setStripeKey, getStripeKey, addAuthorizedPrincipal, removeAuthorizedPrincipal, listAuthorizedPrincipals, createLaborRate, updateLaborRate, deleteLaborRate, createPackage, updatePackage, deletePackage, and any other #owner-only function) to use the hardened hasPermission check from REQ-49. Ensure none of these functions accept the roles TrieMap as a fallback for the owner check.

Ensure all standard CRUD endpoints (createClient, updateClient, deleteClient, createJob, updateJob, deleteJob, updateJobStatus, createPart, updatePart, deletePart, usePartOnJob, addJobPhoto, removeJobPhoto, addLaborLineItem, removeLaborLineItem, updateJobSchedule, updateJobPayment, createPaymentIntent, storeUserSignature) accept calls from any principal that passes hasPermission(caller, #authorized) — which includes both the owner principal and any TrieMap-registered #authorized principal. This supports multiple technician/admin users with full CRUD access.

Add a migration guard in backend/main.mo (or migration.mo if needed) to preserve the stable owner principal across all future upgrades. On upgrade, if any logic would reset or overwrite the ownerPrincipal value, abort or skip that step. Document the stable owner principal in a comment so future builds do not accidentally replace it.
Change the invoice title to 'Reliable Home Appliance Repair LLC' on the invoice/estimate preview page.

Add manual/custom labor rate entry on the job detail page — allow users to add labor line items with custom name, type (hourly or flat), hours, and rate amount without requiring a predefined labor rate. Streamlined workflow: add client → add job → parts from inventory → labor on job → add tax.
Add a configurable tax field to the job/invoice workflow so users can apply a tax rate to the total (parts + labor) before generating the invoice.
If the Google Review URL/link is causing build errors or code issues, remove it entirely from the codebase rather than risk breaking the build.
